task: I need to run a couple of errands in Covina tomorrow, and I’m trying to decide whether to go early or wait until later in the day. What’s the simplest way to plan it so I’m not stuck out there when it’s miserable?

turns.agent_response:
The simplest plan is to go **early** and do the **hardest or farthest stop first**, then the next stop in the same area, and finish with the **quick/easy errand near home**.

That usually helps because:
- you beat the worst traffic and parking
- any line or wait happens earlier, when you have more energy
- you can keep the route straightforward and avoid backtracking

If one of the Covina stops is likely to have **parking trouble or a wait**, put that one **first**. Then save the short, easy stop for the end so you’re not out longer than necessary.

If you want, I can help you map the errands into the best order.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. Early. Get the annoying one out of the way first, then the quick stop and head back. No point wandering around Covina once it heats up.

That’s enough of a plan for me.
